issues.objection: Implication for 7.22 gives a PM a concrete diagnostic to apply. The verse does not require buy-in to the metaphysics; it gives a falsifiable engineering predicate. The STRETCHED tag is honest for this verse: Verse claims Krishna is the actual granter (mayaiva vihitān); engineering analog operates at proximate-vs-upstream attribution scope (the framework is the vehicle; the customer-need-and-substrate is what actually grants the result). Counter-example carves out accidental success without disciplined practice. PM does not have to absorb the metaphysics; the engineering layer is operationally self-contained and the source pack carries the original at honest scope.

notes: Skeptical-PM test for 7.22: can this verse be applied to a roadmap conversation without first signing onto the Gita's frame? Yes — implication gives a concrete next-action; counter-example tells the PM where the analog does not apply. PASS.
